task_title: Reformat slide title and body text in LibreOffice Impress
task_goal: The updated slide formatting is saved in the presentation file.

action_preconditions: The formatted slide is visible with the enlarged underlined title and bold italic body text, and no text is selected.
steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "69_4.pptx". The current slide, labeled as Slide 1 of 1, is titled "The History of Art" and contains a bullet point defining art as a diverse range of human activities creating visual, auditory, or performing artifacts expressing imaginative, conceptual, or technical ideas intended for appreciation based on beauty or emotional power. The interface displays the main menu bar with options like File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. On the left, a slide thumbnail pane shows the current slide. On the right, the Properties panel is open with settings for Slide format (On-screen w: 4:3), Orientation (Landscape), Background (None), and Master Slide (Blank Slide). Layout options are visible below in the Properties panel. The status bar at the bottom indicates the current slide number, zoom level (82%), and language (English USA). The system clock in the top bar shows Mar 25 17:54. The Ubuntu desktop environment is visible with application icons along the left sidebar.
action_after_effects: The presentation remains on the same slide with the formatting preserved after the save command.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Ctrl+S to save the presentation with the updated formatting.
